quick_facts.label: Service classification
quick_facts.value: Store Assistant ‘B’ is governed with Store Assistant ‘A’ under DRDO’s Group ‘C’ recruitment rules; the principal rules were published on 15 September 2022 and amended in 2023.

quick_facts.label: Pay level
quick_facts.value: The post is referenced in current DRDO recruitment rules as Pay Matrix Level 4, ₹25,500–81,100 basic pay. This is the matrix range, not a guaranteed take-home salary.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry basis
quick_facts.value: The Seventh Central Pay Commission’s DRDO cadre table identifies Store Assistant ‘B’ entry as promotion and Limited Departmental Competitive Examination, rather than a general educational-entry post.

quick_facts.label: Identified onward progression
quick_facts.value: Under the 2023 rules for Senior Store Assistant, Store Assistant ‘B’ personnel at Level 4 may be considered for promotion after 10 years of regular service in grade and completion of two to four weeks of relevant training.

sections.heading: How this grade is normally reached
paragraphs: Store Assistant ‘B’ should be understood as a departmental career grade, not assumed to be an open, direct-recruitment role. The official Seventh Central Pay Commission report lists its entry mode in the DRDO cadre as “Promotion and Limited Departmental Competitive Examination.” The applicable DRDO Group ‘C’ rules for Store Assistant ‘A’ and ‘B’ were issued in 2022 and amended in 2023; applicants and serving staff should use the current DRDO vacancy notice and departmental instructions for the precise eligibility, vacancy quota, examination, and seniority conditions in force at the time.

The related Store Assistant ‘A’ cadre is the practical feeder context: the 2023 amendment provides for a five-percent non-selection promotion channel into Store Assistant ‘A’ from eligible Level-1 Group ‘C’ staff with three years’ regular service and Class 12 qualification. That provision does not itself establish eligibility for Store Assistant ‘B’; it illustrates why candidates should distinguish the A-grade entry route from the B-grade internal advancement route.
bullets: Monitor DRDO’s official vacancies portal and the relevant departmental circulars rather than relying on coaching-site summaries.

For serving staff, verify regular-service counting, departmental-examination requirements, training, vacancy year, and any reservation or seniority provisions in the notice applicable to the particular promotion process.

sections.heading: DRDO work setting
paragraphs: A Store Assistant ‘B’ works within the Ministry of Defence’s DRDO environment. DRDO describes itself as a network of around 41 laboratories and five DRDO Young Scientist Laboratories, spanning fields including aeronautics, armaments, electronics, combat vehicles, missiles, naval systems, life sciences, materials, computing, training, information systems and agriculture. Consequently, the operational setting can vary substantially by laboratory or establishment and the materials, documentation, and support requirements of its programme.
bullets: Expect a government defence-research establishment setting rather than a single uniform workplace.
The particular laboratory or establishment determines the local work processes and the types of stores supported.

sections.heading: Progression beyond Store Assistant ‘B’
paragraphs: The clearest rule-based next step identified in the current store-cadre rules is Senior Store Assistant, a Level-6 post. The 2023 Recruitment Rules provide that 40% of Senior Store Assistant vacancies are filled by promotion, failing which by deputation. The feeder provision expressly includes Store Assistant ‘B’ at Level 4 with 10 years’ regular service in the grade and successful completion of two to four weeks of relevant training from CEPTAM or another recognised training institute.

The same rules also show that Senior Store Assistant is a mixed-recruitment post: 60% direct recruitment and 40% promotion, failing which deputation. Promotion depends on the rule conditions and vacancies; completing the service period or training does not by itself guarantee appointment.
bullets: Build and retain documented regular service in the grade.
Complete the prescribed relevant training when made available or required.
Check the applicable promotion notice, since vacancy distribution and procedural requirements can change through later rules or notifications.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As of this review, the cited recruitment rules place Store Assistant ‘B’ at Level 4 in the Central Government pay matrix, whose stated range is ₹25,500–81,100. This is basic pay determined by the appropriate matrix cell and pay-fixation rules, not an all-inclusive monthly salary figure.

Actual gross and take-home pay are variable. They depend on the employee’s matrix cell, applicable dearness allowance, house-rent allowance or government accommodation, transport allowance, location, deductions, tax, and other service-specific entitlements. For that reason, a single “in-hand salary” figure should not be treated as canonical for this node.
bullets: Use ₹25,500 as the Level-4 entry cell in the cited pay matrix, not as a universal net-pay figure.
Recheck Department of Expenditure orders and the individual appointment or pay-fixation order for current allowance rates and entitlement.
